mnemonics: TREND: Track Resource Estimates for Necessary Demand. This uses the first letter of each word in the acronym to help recall the core purpose of trend prediction.
keyPoints: Analyze historical data to identify patterns and project future resource requirements.
Consider both business growth trends (e.g., new users, transactions) and technology trends (e.g., increasing data storage needs).
Use a combination of quantitative methods (like time-series analysis) and qualitative methods (like expert judgment) for accurate predictions.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on linear growth and ignoring potential exponential or seasonal spikes in demand.
Failing to account for new projects or business initiatives that will significantly alter the demand on IT resources.
Relying solely on automated tools without applying business context and human oversight to the predictions.
